mismatch_triage.justification: Both models can be integrated through the fabm kernel, which manages the synchronization internally.
Error handling can be managed through fabm coupling checks and gotm time controls.
Both models can be synchronized through the fabm kernel.
Both models support fortran, which can be used for integration.
The assumptions about horizontal homogeneity and turbulence drivers are incompatible.
The descriptions are similar enough to be integrated through the fabm framework.
The model versions do not match exactly, which could cause compatibility issues.
The software specifications and requirements are not compatible.
Both models can be aligned to multi-year temporal coverage.

mismatches.root_cause: Incompatible assumptions about horizontal homogeneity and turbulence drivers.

evidence.A_value: horizontal homogeneity; turbulence driven by wind, buoyancy, bottom shear; 1d vertical structure
evidence.B_value: ecology responds to physical drivers light, mixing, temperature; trophic groups, sediment modeled mechanistically; no horizontal heterogeneity

mismatches.why_it_blocks_integration: The incompatible assumptions about horizontal homogeneity and turbulence drivers prevent seamless integration.
mismatches.proposed_fix: Reconcile the assumptions by adopting a common set of assumptions that both models can work with.

rules: Adopt a common set of assumptions for horizontal homogeneity and turbulence drivers.
data_transform.example: Assume horizontal homogeneity and turbulence driven by wind, buoyancy, and bottom shear.

steps: Implement common assumptions in both models.

yaml_patches.A: assumptions: horizontal homogeneity; turbulence driven by wind, buoyancy, bottom shear; 1d vertical structure
yaml_patches.B: assumptions: horizontal homogeneity; turbulence driven by wind, buoyancy, bottom shear; 1d vertical structure
yaml_patches.AB: assumptions: horizontal homogeneity; turbulence driven by wind, buoyancy, bottom shear; 1d vertical structure

validation: Verify that both models can operate with the common assumptions.
Test the integrated model to ensure it behaves as expected.

mismatches.root_cause: Model versions do not match exactly.
mismatches.why_it_blocks_integration: Incompatible model versions can cause compatibility issues and unexpected behavior.
mismatches.proposed_fix: Upgrade or downgrade the models to a common version that is compatible with the integration specification.

rules: Upgrade or downgrade the models to a common version.

steps: Test the integrated model to ensure compatibility.

validation: Verify that both models are running the same version.
Test the integrated model to ensure it behaves as expected.

mismatches.why_it_blocks_integration: Incompatible software environments can prevent the models from running together.
mismatches.proposed_fix: Ensure both models can run in a common software environment by installing the necessary libraries and compilers.

data_transform.example: Install netcdf libraries and a fortran compiler in the fabm environment.

steps: Test the integrated model to ensure compatibility.

yaml_patches.A: software_specification_and_requirements: netcdf, fortran compiler, fabm environment
yaml_patches.B: software_specification_and_requirements: netcdf, fortran compiler, fabm environment
yaml_patches.AB: software_specification_and_requirements: netcdf, fortran compiler, fabm environment

validation: Verify that both models can run in the common software environment.
Test the integrated model to ensure it behaves as expected.
